perturbation.original_text: Each party considers all of its Confidential Information and Intellectual Property to be propriety. All of the disclosing party's Confidential Information and Intellectual Property shall at all times, and throughout the world, remain the property of the disclosing party exclusively, and all applicable rights in patents, copyrights and trade secrets shall remain in the disclosing party exclusively. The receiving party promises and agrees to 32 <PAGE> receive and hold the Confidential Information and Intellectual Property in confidence.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party considers all of its Confidential Information and Intellectual Property to be propriety. All of the disclosing party's Confidential Information shall at all times, and throughout the world, remain the property of the disclosing party exclusively, and all applicable rights in patents, copyrights and trade secrets shall remain in the disclosing party exclusively. However, Intellectual Property can be used by the receiving party within the scope of the program. The receiving party promises and agrees to 32 <PAGE> receive and hold the Confidential Information and Intellectual Property in confidence.
perturbation.explanation: In section IV, the original text clearly states that all 'Intellectual Property' of the disclosing party remains exclusively their property. The modification then states that the receiving party can use it within the scope of the program, which is a contradiction. This creates uncertainty about the ownership and usage rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: IV. Nondisclosure Obligations Each party considers all of its Confidential Information and Intellectual Property to be propriety. All of the disclosing party's Confidential Information shall at all times, and throughout the world, remain the property of the disclosing party exclusively, and all applicable rights in patents, copyrights and trade secrets shall remain in the disclosing party exclusively. However, Intellectual Property can be used by the receiving party within the scope of the program. The receiving party promises and agrees to 32 <PAGE> receive and hold the Confidential Information and Intellectual Property in confidence.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that all Intellectual Property remains exclusively the property of the disclosing party. The changed text adds a clause stating that the receiving party can use the Intellectual Property within the scope of the program. This directly contradicts the earlier statement of exclusive ownership and creates a strong in-text contradiction, impacting usage rights.

perturbation.original_text: Each party hereby agrees and acknowledges that no license, either express or implied, is hereby granted with respect to any of the Confidential Information and Intellectual Property.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party hereby agrees and acknowledges that a limited license, for the duration of the Program, is granted with respect to Intellectual Property. For Confidential Information, no license, either express or implied, is hereby granted.
perturbation.explanation: Original text in Section V states that no license, express or implied, is granted for either Confidential Information or Intellectual Property. By modifying it to say that a limited license *is* granted for Intellectual Property directly contradicts the initial statement, and thus it creates an internal contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: V. No Right to Confidential Information and Intellectual Property Each party hereby agrees and acknowledges that a limited license, for the duration of the Program, is granted with respect to Intellectual Property. For Confidential Information, no license, either express or implied, is hereby granted. Each party further agrees that all inventions, improvements, copyrightable works and designs relating to machines, methods, compositions, or products of disclosing party directly resulting from or relating to the Confidential Information and Intellectual Property and the right to market, use, license and franchise the Confidential Information and Intellectual Property or the ideas, concepts, methods or practices embodied therein shall be the exclusive property of the disclosing party, and the receiving party has no right or title thereto.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that no license is granted for Intellectual Property. The changed text explicitly states that a limited license *is* granted for Intellectual Property, directly contradicting the initial statement. The contradicted text repeats the changed text regarding licensing of intellectual property, therefore the initial change introduces a strong in-text contradiction. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Each party's obligations with respect to each item of the Confidential Information and Intellectual Property which it learns or receives from the other prior to the date of termination of the Agreement shall terminate three (3) years after the date of disclosure of such time of Confidential Information and Intellectual Property.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party's obligations with respect to each item of Confidential Information terminate three (3) years after the date of disclosure. However, obligations related to Intellectual Property shall continue indefinitely, even after termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By stating that obligations regarding 'Intellectual Property' continue indefinitely after termination of the agreement, the modified text contradicts itself. The original stated a three year limit for Intellectual Property and Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: IX. Term and Termination. This Agreement shall commence on the date first written above and shall automatically terminate three (3) years thereafter; however, either party may terminate this Agreement at any time prior thereto upon thirty (30) days prior written notice to the other party. Each party's obligations with respect to each item of Confidential Information terminate three (3) years after the date of disclosure. However, obligations related to Intellectual Property shall continue indefinitely, even after termination of this Agreement. Immediately after termination of this Agreement, each party shall return to the other party all of the other party's Confidential Information and Intellectual Property which was disclosed in, or is then represented in, tangible form.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that obligations related to Intellectual Property continue indefinitely, even after termination of the agreement. This directly contradicts the original text and the contradicted text, which both previously stated that each party's obligations with respect to each item of Confidential Information and Intellectual Property terminate three years after the date of disclosure, including obligations for both Confidential Information and Intellectual Property. The change creates a clear inconsistency and contradiction within the document.

perturbation.original_text: Immediately after termination of this Agreement, each party shall return to the other party all of the other party's Confidential Information and Intellectual Property which was disclosed in, or is then represented in, tangible form.
perturbation.changed_text: Immediately after termination of this Agreement, each party shall return to the other party all of the other party's Confidential Information which was disclosed in, or is then represented in, tangible form. Intellectual Property however, need not to be returned.
perturbation.explanation: In Section IX. The original text enforces that Intellectual Property must be returned after the termination of the agreement. But now in the modified text, we are establishing that Intellectual Property need not be returned, creating an internal contradiction between the two sentences
perturbation.contradicted_text: IX. Term and Termination. This Agreement shall commence on the date first written above and shall automatically terminate three (3) years thereafter; however, either party may terminate this Agreement at any time prior thereto upon thirty (30) days prior written notice to the other party. Each party's obligations with respect to each item of Confidential Information terminate three (3) years after the date of disclosure. However, obligations related to Intellectual Property shall continue indefinitely, even after termination of this Agreement. Immediately after termination of this Agreement, each party shall return to the other party all of the other party's Confidential Information which was disclosed in, or is then represented in, tangible form. Intellectual Property however, need not to be returned.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that both Confidential Information and Intellectual Property must be returned after termination. The changed text explicitly states that Intellectual Property need not be returned. The contradicted text, which is the same as the changed text in this case, also reflects this change, creating a direct contradiction because the document now has conflicting statements about whether Intellectual Property should be returned after termination. The changed text creates a contradiction with the original expectation for Intellectual Property to be returned as outlined in the original text which can be seen in section IX. where Intellectual property should be returned, but now, it doesn't have to be.
